name: Caribou Steak, Garlic Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 3 large Green bell peppers; cut into 1/2-inch strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut meat against the grain into 1/2-inch strips.
2. Pound to tenderize.
3. Make a marinade for the meat by combining soy sauce, bouillon, ginger, garlic, and sherry. Marinate steak overnight in the refrigerator.
4. Dry meat on paper towels.
5. In a wok, heat peanut oil and saute chopped garlic until it turns golden brown. Remove, leaving at least 2 tablespoons of oil in the wok.
6. Add meat to the oil and saute until brown (add just a dash of sherry and sesame oil to the meat while it's browning). Set aside.
7. Make a slurry of cornstarch and water.
8. Pour marinade into a separate pan and add cornstarch. Simmer until thick.
9. Stir-fry green peppers and water chestnuts in the wok.
10. Add meat and marinade sauce. Add a dash of sesame oil to taste.
11. Serve over steamed rice.
